mechanism_narrative: A persistently low or falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate applied to future earnings, lifts equity duration assets, and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when policy is not tightening further.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS is flat or falling but 20-day SPX returns are negative in a majority of observed windows, or rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in CPIAUCSL signals inflation pressure that forces tighter policy expectations and higher real yields, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days once the inflation jump exceeds a material threshold.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4% month-over-month or FEDFUNDS is expected to rise within 1 month
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-threshold episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 standard deviation.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE together with a slowdown in PAYEMS growth signals weakening household income and demand, causing earnings expectations to be marked down and S&P 500 returns to underperform over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising unemployment and weakening payrolls does not coincide with negative 60-day forward SPX returns, or if either proxy alone explains the same effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in RSAFS indicates softer nominal consumer spending and weaker near-term revenue growth for large-cap firms, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month when sales deceleration is broad-based.
failure_condition: Reject if below-trend RSAFS readings fail to precede negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate in triggered periods is not materially worse than 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO reflects production weakness and inventory correction, which compresses cyclical profit expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if falling INDPRO is not followed by below-average 60-day SPX returns, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC remains indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A break above trend in HOUST signals housing demand resilience, supporting construction activity, household wealth, and cyclical earnings, which should raise S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-strength triggers do not precede positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the conditional return distribution is no better than the unconditional distribution.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS accelerates while UNRATE falls, labor-market momentum implies stronger income growth and lower recession risk, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-UNRATE combination does not outperform either series alone on 5-day forward returns, or if positive-return frequency in joint-strength episodes is not above 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a high FEDFUNDS level combined with elevated CPIAUCSL suppresses equity valuations through both discount-rate and inflation channels, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-rate and high-inflation regime does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect disappears once either proxy is controlled for independently.
